model.label: Gemini 3 Flash
model.description: Google • Fast Gemini model optimized for low-latency reasoning, multimodal inputs, and tool use.

pick.rationale: Duke enters the tournament as the top-ranked team in the nation, boasting the #1 adjusted defensive efficiency and a top-5 offense according to both KenPom and Bart Torvik. Siena is significantly outmatched in every statistical category, ranking outside the top 180 in both offensive and defensive efficiency. Given Duke's dominant 31-2 record and elite two-way play, they are overwhelming favorites to advance comfortably against a Siena team that lacks the firepower to penetrate their top-tier defense.

reasoningStep.title: Statistical Dominance
reasoningStep.summary: Duke's elite efficiency rankings across both offense and defense make them overwhelming favorites against a Siena team ranked outside the top 180.
evidence: Duke is ranked #1 in KenPom and Torvik.
Duke has the #1 adjusted defensive efficiency in the country.
Siena is ranked 192nd in KenPom with a negative adjusted efficiency margin.
Duke's record is 31-2 compared to Siena's 23-11.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

result.title: 2025-26 Duke Blue Devils men's basketball team - Wikipedia
result.snippet: The 2025-26 Duke Blue Devils men's basketball team represents Duke University during the 2025-26 NCAA Division I men's basketball season . The Blue Devils are led by fourth-year head coach Jon Scheyer and play their games at Cameron Indoor Stadium in Durham, North Carolina, as a member of the Atlantic Coast Conference.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
